title: spec-readiness
description: The deontic gate for spec cells. Realized as enforcement (hooks + the entailment script), not prose. Defines when a spec may advance to validated and when a decomposition may emit tickets.

rules.obligation: A spec cell advances to `validated` only when a spec-quality signal artifact exists, written by the validation path (the critic), not the authoring agent.
The spec-quality rubric cell must itself be `validated` (deterministic + calibrated) before it gates any spec.
rules.violation: block; no spec binds to an unvalidated rubric

rules.obligation: A spec may emit a ticket batch only when _entailment_check.py passes: full coverage of parent criteria by child cells, every child acceptance bound to a rubric cell, no orphan criteria, partial-order-legal edges.
rules.violation: block; return spec to regenerating with the gap recorded

rules.obligation: A migrated (brownfield) spec earns `validated` by signal through the same rubric as a greenfield spec. Prose completeness is never sufficient.
A validated spec changes only through a ledgered `regenerating` transition; silent edits are forbidden.
rules.violation: block; the edit is rejected and must re-enter via regenerating

note: Workers in spec-author are deny-on-write to protected_paths. The authoring agent never writes the signal that validates its own spec.
